questions.prompt: Honestly — how many days a week will you show up?

options.title: That's the part I struggle with
options.subtitle: Start tiny — daily beats perfect

educationPages.title: Warm up, always
educationPages.body: Five minutes of easy movement raises muscle temperature and tells your joints what's coming. It's the cheapest injury insurance there is — never lift or run completely cold.

educationPages.title: Progressive overload
educationPages.body: Muscles adapt to what you ask of them, then stop. Add a rep, a little weight, or a few minutes each week — tiny consistent increases are the entire secret.

educationPages.title: Rest days build muscle
educationPages.body: Training is the stimulus; growth happens while you recover. Back-to-back hard days on the same muscles just digs a hole. Sleep is the most anabolic thing you do all day.

educationPages.title: Soreness vs. pain
educationPages.body: Dull, symmetrical next-day soreness is normal. Sharp pain, joint pain, or anything one-sided that changes how you move is not — stop and see a physio or doctor rather than pushing through.

products.name: Exercise mat
products.what: A grippy cushioned mat for floor work.
products.why: You'll stretch twice as long when your knees and spine aren't on hard floor.
products.order: Under every stretch, plank and floor exercise.

products.name: Resistance band set
products.what: Loop bands in a few tensions.
products.why: A full strength stimulus that fits in a drawer — perfect for desk resets and travel.
products.order: For pull-aparts, rows and assistance work.

products.name: Foam roller
products.what: A firm foam cylinder for self-massage.
products.why: Eases the tight spots that make you skip tomorrow's session.
products.order: On recovery days, slow passes over calves, quads and upper back.
products.caution: Roll muscle, not joints or your lower spine.
